The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), under {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)}}

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区) up to rev 79268134
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 宗形神社 |画像 = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]大窪193 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=宗形神社}} |祭神 = [[タゴリヒメ|田心姫命]]
[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]
[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宗形神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[一宮地域|大窪]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は次の3柱（[[宗像三女神]]）。 * [[タゴリヒメ|田心姫命]] （たごりひめのみこと） * [[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]] （たぎつひめのみこと） * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]] （いちきしまひめのみこと） == 歴史 == 創建は不詳であるが、[[宗像大社]]（[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]）からの勧請と伝える『日本の神々』宗形神社項。。小高い丘の上に鎮座しているが、丘の南を東西に古代の[[山陽道]]が走っており、当地には[[駅家]]が設置されていた。一帯は古くは入江であり古代からの遺跡が多く残っていることから、当社は社伝通り航海の守護神として勧請されたとも考えうる。社殿の背後には[[円墳]]も残っており、祭祀の古さがうかがえる。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には[[備前国]][[津高郡]]に「宗形神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。なお、備前国には[[赤坂郡]]にも式内社として宗形神社（[[赤磐市]]是里）の記載がある。吉備高原に位置するこの神社と当社とは性格は異なるが、創祀における関係が示唆される。このほか、『[[備前国神名帳]]』の神上金剛寺本に「正四位下 宗形大明神」と記載されている境内説明板。。 明治4年に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列し、明治40年に神饌幣帛料共進神社に指定された。 == 境内 == 本殿の後方には古墳が残る。古墳の形状は[[円墳]]で、直径約15メートル、高さ2メートル。埋葬施設は箱式石棺構造で、内面に赤色顔料が塗られている。 1997年7月に新たに発見され、人骨とともに副葬品が発掘された古墳説明板。。 File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|本殿
後方に古墳。 File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|古墳 == 摂末社 == * 摂社：疫神社 - 祭神：[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]（本社の相殿とする） * 末社：祖霊社 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]大窪193 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[吉備線]] [[備前一宮駅]]から ** 徒歩：約30分 ** レンタサイクル：備前一宮駅前で可能 * 西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本） [[岡山駅]]から ** [[中鉄バス]]（大窪経由芳賀佐山団地線）で「大窪」バス停下車 （下車後徒歩約5分） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 境内説明板 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 岡山県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）岡山市 大窪村項 * 谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 2 山陽 四国』（[[白水社]]）宗形神社項 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4989 宗形神社古墳]|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 宗形神社]（岡山県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むなかたしんしや おかやましきたく}} [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宗像神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]]

[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宗形神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[一宮地域|大窪]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は次の3柱（[[宗像三女神]]）。 * [[タゴリヒメ|田心姫命]] （たごりひめのみこと） * [[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]] （たぎつひめのみこと） * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]] （いちきしまひめのみこと） == 歴史 == 創建は不詳であるが、[[宗像大社]]（[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]）からの勧請と伝える『日本の神々』宗形神社項。。小高い丘の上に鎮座しているが、丘の南を東西に古代の[[山陽道]]が走っており、当地には[[駅家]]が設置されていた。一帯は古くは入江であり古代からの遺跡が多く残っていることから、当社は社伝通り航海の守護神として勧請されたとも考えうる。社殿の背後には[[円墳]]も残っており、祭祀の古さがうかがえる。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には[[備前国]][[津高郡]]に「宗形神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。なお、備前国には[[赤坂郡]]にも式内社として宗形神社（[[赤磐市]]是里）の記載がある。吉備高原に位置するこの神社と当社とは性格は異なるが、創祀における関係が示唆される。このほか、『[[備前国神名帳]]』の神上金剛寺本に「正四位下 宗形大明神」と記載されている境内説明板。。 明治4年に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列し、明治40年に神饌幣帛料共進神社に指定された。 == 境内 == 本殿の後方には古墳が残る。古墳の形状は[[円墳]]で、直径約15メートル、高さ2メートル。埋葬施設は箱式石棺構造で、内面に赤色顔料が塗られている。 1997年7月に新たに発見され、人骨とともに副葬品が発掘された古墳説明板。。 File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|本殿
後方に古墳。 File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|古墳 == 摂末社 == * 摂社：疫神社 - 祭神：[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]（本社の相殿とする） * 末社：祖霊社 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]大窪193 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[吉備線]] [[備前一宮駅]]から ** 徒歩：約30分 ** レンタサイクル：備前一宮駅前で可能 * 西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本） [[岡山駅]]から ** [[中鉄バス]]（大窪経由芳賀佐山団地線）で「大窪」バス停下車 （下車後徒歩約5分） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 境内説明板 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 岡山県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）岡山市 大窪村項 * 谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 2 山陽 四国』（[[白水社]]）宗形神社項 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4989 宗形神社古墳]|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 宗形神社]（岡山県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むなかたしんしや おかやましきたく}} [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宗像神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Okayama City]] [[Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), under {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国}}'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927 CE), under {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|12=draft}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|12=draft}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|12=draft}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|12=draft}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Okayama City]] [[Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=draft}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=draft}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|12=draft}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927 CE), under {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|12=draft}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|12=draft}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|12=draft}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|12=draft}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]]

File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Okayama City]] [[Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|qq=draft}} in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft}} 5 (927 CE), under {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]]

File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Okayama City]] [[Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|qq=draft}} in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft}} 5 (927 CE), under {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]]

Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Okayama City]] [[Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}} in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), under {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province|qq=}}'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]]

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}} in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), under {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province|qq=}}'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]]

Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Munakata shrines]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine |location = [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Okayama City]] [[Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Historical sites in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Munakata shrines]] [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]]

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[JR West|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} -
