{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]] [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}}

Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - Main Shrine |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]] [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}}

"Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}}

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17217272]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]] [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|46.16|N|133|51|33.91|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Munakata Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17217272]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]] [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.696156|N|133.859419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takiribime|qid=Q10932870}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q855478}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=|1=umaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668204}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=|1=Tsudaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146513}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=|1=Akasaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635439}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社|1=Yasaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q692714}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17217272]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]] [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.696156|N|133.859419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takiribime|qid=Q10932870}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q855478}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=|1=umaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668204}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=|1=Tsudaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146513}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=|1=Akasaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635439}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社|1=Yasaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q692714}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17217272]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]] [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.696156|N|133.859419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takiribime|qid=Q10932870}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q855478}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=|1=umaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668204}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=|1=Tsudaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146513}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=|1=Akasaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635439}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社|1=Yasaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q692714}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)]] [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17217272]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]] [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.696156|N|133.859419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takiribime|qid=Q10932870}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q855478}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=|1=umaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668204}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=|1=Tsudaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146513}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=|1=Akasaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635439}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type stone coffin with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社|1=Yasaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q692714}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q17217272}}

[[commons:Category:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)]][[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17217272]] Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Munakata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) shaden.JPG |location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]] [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita Ward]], Ōkubo 193 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.696156|N|133.859419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takiribime|qid=Q10932870}} |Sacred Object = |type= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q855478}} }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōkubo, [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The primary deities are the following three {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is said to have been an extension of [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].Referenced from 'The Gods of Japan,' Munakata Shrine entry. The shrine is situated on a small hill, with the ancient [[Sanyōdō (road)|Sanyō Road]] running east to west along the hill's southern side, indicating the area's historical significance, including a former {{ill|Ekiya|ja|駅家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya|qq=|1=umaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668204}}. The surrounding area, once an inlet with numerous ancient sites, suggests the shrine might have been established as a maritime guardian deity. Behind the shrine stands a preserved circular Kofun (円墳), highlighting the antiquity of the site's rituals. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927 CE), under [[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'s {{ill|Tsukasa District|ja|津高郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukasa District|qq=|1=Tsudaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11146513}} as "Munakata Shrine." Another shrine with the same name is listed under {{ill|Akasaka District, Bizen|ja|赤坂郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka District, Bizen|qq=|1=Akasaka district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635439}}, located in [[Akaiwa City]]'s Koreyama area. Although differing in nature, the two shrines might share connections in their original foundation. Additionally, the {{ill|Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|ja|備前国神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province Jinmyōchō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists it as "Shōshiige Munakata Daimyōjin."On-site explanatory sign. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] and was designated a shrine for offering Imperial ritual offerings in Meiji 40 (1907). == Grounds == Behind the main hall, a circular Kofun (円墳) remains, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters tall. The burial structure is a box-type Sarcophagus with red pigment applied to its interior. Human remains and grave goods were discovered during excavations in 1997.On-site explanatory sign for the Kofun. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) honden.JPG|Main Hall
With the Kofun behind. File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) heiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama) kofun.JPG|Kofun == Subsidiary Shrines == * Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Ekijinja|ja|疫神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社|1=Yasaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q692714}} - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (also worshiped in the main shrine hall) * Subordinate Shrine: Ancestral Spirit Shrine (祖霊社) == Access == '''Address''' * Ōkubo 193, Kita-ku, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * From [[Bizen Ichinomiya Station]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]): ''' Walk: Approximately 30 minutes ''' Bicycle rental available at Bizen Ichinomiya Station * From [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]: ''' Take the [[Chūtetsu Bus]] (Ōkubo Route to Haga-Sayama Housing Complex Line) and disembark at "Ōkubo" stop (about a 5-minute walk from there) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * On-site explanatory sign * {{ill|Japan Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place name Series|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} - "Okayama Prefecture: Ōkubo Village" entry (Heibonsha) * Edited by Kenichi Tanikawa, 'The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - Volume 2: Sanyō, Shikoku' (Hakusuisha), Munakata Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=07039 Munakata Shrine] (Okayama Shrine Association) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|version=79268134|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:宗形神社 (岡山市北区)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kita-ku, Okayama)}} {{wikidata link|Q17217272}}
